summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard captures a spring ’77 show brimming with killer Estimated Prophet→Eyes, Lazy Lightnin’→Supplication and soaring Stella Blue
review: The recording is an excellent SBD source – crisp, deep bass and virtually no mix problems – letting the band’s tight 1977 performance shine.  Reviewers rave about the explosive Estimated Prophet into Eyes of the World, the fiery Lazy Lightnin’/Supplication sequence and a sublime Stella Blue, while even the more modest moments (Deal, Jack Straw) feel solid.  Overall the show is held up as one of the best of the May‑77 run.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, most giving 5 stars and praising both performance and audio; a few 4‑star notes do not outweigh the strong consensus.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp mix with deep, clear bass; occasional faint “ghost music” noted but never detracts. No drop‑outs or balance issues reported.

standout_songs: Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World
poor_songs: Deal (a few reviewers found it flat)
show_quality.setlist_flow: Setlists flow effortlessly – a strong opener with Deal, an explosive middle segment highlighted by Estimated/Eyes and Lazy>Supplication, and a soaring second set that builds to The Other One and Stella Blue.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for flawless guitar tone, soaring solos (especially on Estimated Prophet and Eyes), and expressive vocals – described as angelic and ahead of the beat.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass noted as booming and solid; a few reviewers singled out his low‑end power on this night.
band_member_comments.Bob: Credited with tight rhythm work, especially on Jack‑A‑Roe where his guitar “gets to add” and drives the groove.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is highlighted for sharp, unplugged playing on Estimated Prophet and overall strong keyboard textures throughout.
